description: A JIT type confusion vulnerability exists in V8's Maglev compiler due to missing bytecode size accounting for small eagerly-inlined functions. In MaglevGraphBuilder::TryBuildInlineCall, when ShouldEagerInlineCall() returns true (for tiny functions like noop()), execution takes an early-exit path directly to BuildEagerInlineCall() without calling graph()->add_inlined_bytecode_size(). As a result, the cumulative bytecode size guard in CanInlineCall() is never triggered, allowing an unbounded number of small functions to be eagerly inlined. Each inlined call pushes an entry onto graph()->inlined_functions(), and the inlining ID is derived from this vector's size. The InliningIdField in SourcePosition is a 16-bit BitField64 (max value 65535), and SetInliningId stores id+1 in it. After more than ~65534 inlined function entries (achievable with a function containing 70,000 calls to a tiny function), the inlining ID overflows this field. In debug builds, this triggers the DCHECK(is_valid(value)) assertion in BitField::encode. In release builds, the SourcePosition inlining metadata is silently corrupted (ID wraps around), causing the JIT compiler to confuse the identity of inlined call sites and enabling type confusion during compilation.

fixes.evidence_text: Not seeing any issues related to this fix at this time, please [https://crrev.com/c/6732846](<https://crrev.com/c/6732846>) to 13.9 and 13.8 by EOD tomorrow / Friday so this fix can be included in the next update of M138 Stable, and M139 Stable RC being cut on Tuesday
fixes.description: Local patch exported from https://crrev.com/c/6732846 for reproducible fixed-image builds.
